transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: Okay let's start with the velocity review. We closed 31 story points last sprint, target was 36. Marco what happened with the WebSocket work?

transcript.speaker: dev_marco
transcript.text: The memory leak is more complex than we thought. I can reproduce it under load but not locally. I need access to production heap snapshots to go further. Alice can you approve that? I'll need the runbook updated too since right now there's no procedure for attaching a heap profiler in prod without downtime.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Yes, I'll approve heap snapshot access by end of day. Marco can you own the runbook update? Have it done before you take the snapshot so we have the procedure documented first.

transcript.speaker: dev_marco
transcript.text: Will do, I'll have the runbook ready by Tuesday morning.

transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: Good. Priya — the GDPR deletion pipeline?

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: Still blocked on the schema migration fix. I've identified the broken table reference. The fix is two lines but I need someone to pair with me on testing because the deletion logic touches 6 tables and I don't want to get this wrong. Can Lena pair with me on Wednesday?

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: Yes, Wednesday works. Let's say 10 AM and plan for a full morning.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Great. Priya and Lena — that pairing session is a must-do this week. Please block calendars now. After the fix is merged I want Priya to manually process the 47 backlogged requests with monitoring on — don't just run the script and walk away.

transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: The SSL cert is also on the radar. Alice who owns that?

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: That's me and Raj from infra. We need to manually renew and fix the Certbot config. I'll do that Thursday. Raj needs to send me the CSR before Wednesday noon otherwise we don't have time to get it validated before the cert expires. I need to chase Raj on that today.

transcript.speaker: dev_kenji
transcript.text: Quick note on the Next.js hydration errors — I've found the root cause, it's the date library. Fix is ready in a draft PR. I just need a 30-minute review slot from Lena since she knows the SSR setup best.

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: Send me the PR link, I'll review it before standup tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: For sprint 25 planning — the big new item is the board presentation prep. Alice mentioned we need a post-mortem on the WebSocket incident written before Monday. Who's writing that?

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Marco writes the technical sections, I'll write the executive summary. Marco, I need your sections by Saturday noon at the latest so I can assemble the doc over the weekend. Please include timeline, root cause, blast radius and remediation steps.

transcript.speaker: dev_marco
transcript.text: Got it, Saturday noon.

transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: One more thing — the Globex Corp escalation on latency. Alice, they're a renewal risk.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: I know. I need to reply to Alex Torres today with a named owner and timeline. Priya, after the GDPR fix lands, can you own the Globex p99 investigation? It's the pgBouncer PR that should fix it but we need someone dedicated to monitoring after deploy.

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: Yes, I can take that. I'll need Marco's help interpreting the DB metrics though.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Agreed. Let's wrap up. Action items: Marco — runbook by Tuesday, post-mortem sections by Saturday. Priya — GDPR fix Wednesday, Globex monitoring after deploy. Lena — pair with Priya Wednesday 10 AM, review Kenji's PR tomorrow. Kenji — send PR link to Lena today. Me — approve heap access today, reply to Globex today, chase Raj for CSR by today, SSL renewal Thursday.

transcript.speaker: pm_bob
transcript.text: Great. I'll send the sprint 25 board after this meeting. Thanks everyone.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: We need to make a decision on the observability stack. Lena, walk us through the options.

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: Three options on the table. Option 1: keep the current fragmented setup — separate tools for logs, metrics and traces with no correlation. Option 2: Grafana stack — Loki for logs, Mimir for metrics, Tempo for traces, all correlated via trace IDs. Option 3: Datadog — full observability in one product but $4,200/month at our current scale. My recommendation is Option 2. We can self-host on the existing k8s cluster.

transcript.speaker: infra_raj
transcript.text: I'd support Option 2 as well. I can provision the Grafana stack in staging by end of next week. The main work is configuring the OTel collector to fan out to Loki and Tempo simultaneously.

transcript.speaker: dev_marco
transcript.text: What about the alert routing? Right now PagerDuty routes based on Prometheus alerts. Does that change?

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: No, Mimir is Prometheus-compatible. PagerDuty integration stays the same. We just add the Grafana datasources.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Decision made — we go with Option 2, Grafana stack. Raj can you own the provisioning? I need staging ready for evaluation by June 27.

transcript.speaker: infra_raj
transcript.text: I can do June 27. I'll need Lena to write the OTel collector config since she knows the service topology.

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: I'll have the collector config ready by Monday. I'll put it in the infra repo under observability/.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Good. One more thing — the OpenTelemetry tracing ticket JIRA-1007 is still open for task-worker and notification-service. Lena, now that we've decided on the stack, can you scope out the instrumentation work and break it into subtasks this week? I want a clear set of subtasks in Jira by Friday so we can plan it properly in sprint 25.

transcript.speaker: dev_lena
transcript.text: Yes, I'll break it down by Friday. Probably 3-4 subtasks — one per service plus one for the collector config.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Perfect. Also Raj, the internal CA cert that expired this morning — I assume you're on it?

transcript.speaker: infra_raj
transcript.text: Yes, rolling it now. New cert is being issued. Should be restored within the hour. I'll send an all-clear to the incident channel.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Great. Send the post-restoration report to me before EOD so I have it for the board packet. Raj — make sure you also add an automated expiry alert for all internal certs — this cannot happen silently again.

transcript.speaker: infra_raj
transcript.text: Agreed. I'll set up cert-expiry Prometheus alerts by end of this week.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: How are you finding the GDPR work? Feels like it's been blocking for a while.

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: It's stressful honestly. The legal team keeps adding scope. Now they want me to also document every system that touches EU data, not just fix the deletion pipeline. And I've not had time to finish the payment module test coverage either.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: The documentation ask is legitimate — legal emailed me about it too. I need that list from you by June 28 for the GDPR audit. Can you timebox it to one day and then hand it off to me to review?

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: One day is fine if I can deprioritise the payment coverage work until after the GDPR deadline.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Agreed. Payment coverage moves to the week of June 30. I'll update Jira. What else is on your mind?

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: I wanted to raise — I think we need a dedicated oncall rotation. Right now when production incidents happen at 2 AM it always seems to fall on me or Marco. It's not sustainable.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: You're right. I've been meaning to formalise the oncall rotation. I'll set up a PagerDuty schedule this week with a fair rotation across the team. I'll have a draft rotation to everyone by Thursday so people can flag conflicts before it goes live.

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: That would make a huge difference. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: One more item — the Stripe API migration. I got an email from them. Can you look at the breaking changes in their migration guide and give me a rough estimate for the work? I need to decide if it goes in the next sprint or gets pushed to Q4.

transcript.speaker: dev_priya
transcript.text: I'll read the migration guide today and send you an estimate by EOD. I remember the Stripe integration from when I built it — the Payment Intent changes are the tricky part.

transcript.speaker: alice_kumar
transcript.text: Perfect. And one last thing — please update your out-of-office before you leave for the conference next week. Put Marco as backup contact for anything urgent.
